name: Hazelnut-Chocolate Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine half-and-half, chocolate, and sugar. Place over medium-low heat, stirring frequently until the chocolate melts.
2. Beat the egg yolks in a small bowl. Whisk in about 1/2 cup of the chocolate mixture. Stir the egg mixture into the soup.
3. Simmer, stirring frequently until the soup thickens slightly, about 5 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and stir in the liqueurs.
5. To serve hot, ladle into warmed bowls, add a dollop of whipped cream, sprinkle with hazelnuts, and serve immediately. Alternatively, pour into a container, tightly cover, and refrigerate for up to 5 days. Reheat slowly before garnishing and serving.
6. To serve cold, pour into a container, tightly cover, and refrigerate until chilled, at least 2 hours or as long as 5 days. Remove from the refrigerator about 20 minutes before serving. Ladle into chilled bowls and garnish as for hot soup.
7. Serves 6 as a brunch soup course or dessert.
